title: GAME Detective Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: [GAME] Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by GVman » Sun Sep 07, 2008 12:12 am What in God's name is this game about? The whole detective thing in the title is somewhat off-putting.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Sep 07, 2008 12:14 am I want to say it has something to do with EVA-04. I haven't really read into it I just looked at it briefly. If you search around I'm sure you could figure it out.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 07, 2008 1:47 am I've beaten the first chapter. You play as Shinji Ikari, hired as a detective investigating murders at NERV, a junior high school. Also, there's this tentacle monster girl person thing. And you occasionally pilot your Evangelion to fight giant Angel things.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Sep 07, 2008 11:22 am Also, there's this tentacle monster girl person thing

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Sep 08, 2008 3:26 pm It's the most recent Eva game that has voice acting work done by the seiyuu and was released a few months before Evangelion 1.0 was. Hope that helps somewhat. And yes it's an AU.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:08 pm Why have I never heard of this before? Detective Evangelion? That's just wierd.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 13, 2009 7:31 am Humbly requesting that anyone who knows anything about this game do something about our total lack of coverage . Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Apr 13, 2009 9:24 am Sounds like what would happen if Detective Conan went to sleep after an EVA marathon with lots of spicy food to go along with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Apr 13, 2009 12:04 pm I remember I hear something about this before on Gamefaqs and it feel like Clue mixed with Eva in it. Does anyone have some gameplay videos?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Apr 13, 2009 12:42 pm Reichu wrote: Humbly requesting that anyone who knows anything about this game do something about our total lack of coverage . Thank you.

[]: [GAMES] TDSA presents: Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon Mar 28, 2011 9:21 pm Ever heard of the obscure Eva game mentioned above? Well neither did I until WalrusGuy made a YouTube Poop/Rick Roll with it and further learned about it on the main site's Wiki page. So yeah, over a year ago someone uploaded it and thanks to me using Google Translate, some copy and pasting, and gut work I found a walkthrough of the cinematics of it on YouTube (and I have no idea what order they're in): http://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=%E5%90%8D%E6%8E%A2%E5%81%B5%E3%82%A8%E3%83%B4%E3%82%A1%E3%83%B3%E3%82%B2%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AA%E3%83%B3&aq=f Seriously, there's some pretty screwy sh*t in that game and I've only seen the stuff on the first page (it goes to page 4). I can't read Japanese so I can't put them in any order let alone understand what the hell they're talking about. Now they did answer a couple of mysteries from the series. We find out that Tabris can control more than one Eva unit at the same time AND he fuses with Unit Beta to try and reform Adam, even emitting an Anti-AT Field...... Although somehow Lilith, her cross, the floor containing her cross, and the main Eva trio are still around despite everything else looking apocalyptic. No joke: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZLcnNKmpAdQ Edit: I put this in the wrong section, could someone move this to the regular "Everything Else Eva" forum?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 28, 2011 9:35 pm The game's not really obscure, it was released in 2006 or 2007 (around the time Eva 1.0 was in theaters, I think). I've only seen a few opening clips but the game looks somewhat interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 28, 2011 10:18 pm Previous thread (merged) Game booklet scans: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6416

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Mon Mar 28, 2011 11:23 pm Sounds pretty interesting. Perhaps there should be a translation project if we can get our hands on a text dump?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Tue Mar 29, 2011 1:05 pm I'd like to know more about the angel hybrids here; so far I've only seen the possessed child, the oni, and the bowling pins ones.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Nov 06, 2015 2:46 am Sorry to dust off this thread after so long, but I've been watching a playthrough of the game on youtube and wanted to share some of the information I've been able to glean. The videos are available here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=P ... B6FC88041A . I think Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion linked to it further up the thread as well. The game starts off with some pretty straightforward murder mysteries, but from Case 5 on, the revelations come fast and heavy and it is really hard to understand what the hell is going on without being able to speak Japanese. In some ways Detective Eva might actually be darker than the original. Adam, Lilith, and Kaworu are the only returning Angels, with five new ones being added to the mix. Weirdly, Kaworu is still identified as the 17th Angel for some reason. Edit: I get it now! They counted each of the bowling pins as one Angel. That brings the total up to 17. Clever! Here's a rundown of the game's cases with some of the major events. Credit to riotgear for the booklet scans: Case 1: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: [GAME] Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by MarqFJA87 » Sun Feb 23, 2020 10:32 pm Necroing this thread to add some information on the original characters introduced in this game (all of whom are murder victims), which I've managed to glean from the Japanese Wikipedia (specifically the article for NGE characters' game section ) using my limited knowledge of Japanese. Included are images of the characters in question that I've acquired via screen capture from Higemaru's playthrough or from previously linked Imgur galleries; in case of the former, I'll admit right now that I haven't thoroughly scoured the playthrough due to time constraints, instead hovering the cursor over the video progress bar to peek at the preview images to quickly locate the scenes where the murders are first shown. #1: Natsuko Kako (加古ナツコ) Corpse  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: [GAME] Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Feb 29, 2020 9:57 am That's some neat niche info, thanks for sharing this! I'll get to adding them to the extracanonical stuff section of the wiki one of these days, someone smack me over the head if I don't

[]: Re: [GAME] Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by MarqFJA87 » Mon Mar 16, 2020 6:37 pm The four victims have been added to the Extracanonical Characters page, but the image links are broken. Are we waiting for better images than the ones I have provided?

[]: Re: [GAME] Detective Evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Mar 17, 2020 7:41 am No, those were just technical difficulties that I didn't yet have the time to troubleshoot
